title: Hodgkin-Huxley Neuron Model Parameter Estimation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Hodgkin-Huxley Neuron Model Parameter Estimation: Hodgkin-Huxley parameter estimation: infer ion channel conductances and gating kinetics from patch-clamp recordings. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ode.hodgkin_huxley…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=5 with effective condition number kappa_eff~1000; channel_noise_stochastic, dendritic_morphology_neglect set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.world_state_x: HH parameters {g_Na, g_K, g_L, E_Na, E_K, E_L, C_m, tau kinetics}
E.observation_y: membrane voltage V(t) and ionic currents I(t) from voltage-clamp experiments

W.regime: Existence of the recovered HH_parameter_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 1000); channel_noise_stochastic dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: voltage RMSE < 2 mV for calibrated HH model. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~1000. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |channel_noise_stochastic|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_timesteps * N_channels) per ODE integration per iteration
